methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy, a Series D climate and energy company founded by Kunal Girotra, has raised $300M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including Redwood Materials, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $300M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $450M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C)—produces a median of $2.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $6.0B. The peer set reflects material heterogeneity in maturity and progress within the climate-tech space.

Triangulating both methods yields a valuation range of $382M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.2B. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around Lunar Energy's relative positioning within the peer cohort: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to deployed capital, while the peer median incorporates market pricing of comparable private capital rounds. Key sensitivities include the company's actual technology differentiation relative to Redwood Materials and KoBold Metals, as well as near-term capital needs and exit timing.
